title: Caresono PadScan HD Series Bladder Scanner - Bladder not detected or low-confidence scan
description: Troubleshooting bladder not detected, poor scan confidence, unclear bladder outline, or inconsistent bladder volume readings on a PadScan HD Series scanner.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not continue repeated scanning if it delays needed clinical care. Confirm the patient is stable and that clinical staff are aware the scan result is unreliable. If urinary retention is strongly suspected and the scanner cannot provide a reliable result, advise clinical staff to use an alternate assessment method or another bladder scanner. Expected outcome: Patient care decisions are not based on a low-confidence or failed scan result. Why it matters: A bladder scanner is a decision-support device. Inaccurate or failed scans can delay catheterization, urinary retention evaluation, or other care.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Problem
steps.instructions: Confirm exactly what clinical staff are seeing. Ask whether the issue is bladder not detected. Ask whether the issue is a low-confidence scan result. Ask whether the issue is repeated low volume despite symptoms. Ask whether the issue is unstable or inconsistent volume readings. Ask whether the issue is poor image quality. Ask whether the scan failure occurs only on certain patients. Ask whether the scan failure started after cleaning, charging, or software startup. Expected outcome: The failure pattern is identified before assuming a device fault. If the issue was caused by incorrect scan technique or a one-time patient positioning problem, correct the process and stop.

steps.title: Confirm the Scanner Powers On Normally
steps.instructions: Check the basic operating condition of the PadScan HD unit. Power the scanner on. Confirm the display starts normally and there are no system errors, startup freezes, battery warnings, or probe recognition messages. Expected outcome: The scanner reaches the normal scanning screen without errors. If the scanner does not start normally, troubleshoot the power, battery, or startup issue first.

steps.title: Check Battery Charge and Power Status
steps.instructions: Low battery or unstable power can affect device behavior. Confirm the battery has adequate charge. Place the scanner on its charger or connect approved power if available. Allow the unit to charge if the battery is low. Repeat the scan after stable power is confirmed. Expected outcome: The scanner operates normally without low-battery warnings. If the issue resolves after charging, document the battery condition and return the device to service after confirming repeat scans are reliable.

steps.title: Inspect the Probe and Cable
steps.instructions: The probe is a common external source of poor image quality or failed detection. Inspect the probe face for cracks, residue, dried gel, scratches, impact damage, or contamination. Check the probe cable for kinks, cuts, strain, loose connection, or damage near the connector. Confirm the probe is fully seated if the model uses a removable probe connection. Expected outcome: The probe and cable appear clean, intact, and properly connected. If damage is found, remove the scanner from service and send it for repair or probe evaluation.

steps.title: Clean the Probe Surface
steps.instructions: Residue on the probe can interfere with ultrasound transmission. Clean the probe face according to facility-approved cleaning procedures. Remove dried gel, disinfectant residue, lint, or buildup from the scanning surface. Dry the probe as required before reuse. Expected outcome: The probe face is clean and smooth before scanning. If cleaning restores reliable bladder detection, document the cleaning performed and return the device to service.

steps.title: Verify Proper Ultrasound Gel Use
steps.instructions: Insufficient gel can cause poor acoustic coupling and low-confidence scans. Confirm that enough approved ultrasound gel is used between the probe and the patient. Make sure the gel is not dried out, contaminated, or applied too thinly. Repeat the scan with fresh gel. Expected outcome: The scan image improves and the bladder is detected more reliably. If the issue resolves, the cause was likely poor probe-to-skin coupling.

steps.title: Confirm Patient Positioning and Scan Location
steps.instructions: Incorrect probe placement can cause the scanner to miss the bladder. Confirm the patient is positioned appropriately, usually supine or as clinically appropriate. Place the probe in the lower abdomen above the pubic bone. Aim the probe toward the bladder, not straight down at the abdomen. Avoid scanning too high, too lateral, or over clothing, dressings, pads, or excess bedding. Expected outcome: The scanner detects the bladder with improved confidence. If repositioning resolves the issue, document scan technique correction.

steps.title: Check for Patient-Specific Factors
steps.instructions: Some patients are harder to scan accurately. Ask clinical staff if the patient has obesity or large abdominal tissue depth. Ask clinical staff if the patient has abdominal wounds, dressings, or surgical sites. Ask clinical staff if the patient is pregnant. Ask clinical staff if the patient has ascites or abdominal fluid. Ask clinical staff if the patient has a pelvic mass. Ask clinical staff if the patient had recent bladder surgery. Ask clinical staff if the patient has a urinary catheter in place. Ask clinical staff if the patient has very low bladder volume. Ask clinical staff if the patient is unable to lie still. Expected outcome: Possible patient-related causes are identified. If the issue occurs only on difficult-to-scan patients, the scanner may be functioning normally but producing limited results due to clinical conditions.

steps.title: Repeat the Scan Using a Controlled Technique
steps.instructions: A single scan may not be enough to judge the device. Perform multiple scans using correct placement, adequate gel, and steady probe pressure. Compare whether the bladder outline and volume are consistent. Do not press excessively, as this may distort anatomy or patient comfort. Expected outcome: Repeated scans produce similar results with acceptable confidence. If repeat scans are consistent and clinically reasonable, the scanner may be functioning properly.

steps.title: Compare Against Another Known-Good Scanner if Available
steps.instructions: Comparison helps separate device failure from patient or technique issues. Scan the same patient or a test scenario with another known-good bladder scanner if available. Compare detection confidence and volume trend. Expected outcome: Both scanners produce similar general results. If the PadScan HD repeatedly fails while another scanner works reliably under the same conditions, suspect a PadScan probe, calibration, software, or internal imaging issue.

steps.title: Check Settings, Patient Mode, and Scan Type
steps.instructions: Incorrect settings can affect scan confidence or volume calculation. Confirm the correct patient type, scan mode, or gender-related setting is selected if applicable to the unit’s workflow. Check whether the scanner is in the expected bladder scan mode and not a demo, training, or alternate mode. Expected outcome: The scanner is configured correctly for the patient and exam type. If correcting the setting resolves the issue, document the incorrect setting and return the scanner to service after confirming operation.

steps.title: Restart the Scanner
steps.instructions: Temporary software faults can cause low-confidence results or scan failure. Power the scanner off. Wait briefly. Power it back on and allow it to fully restart. Repeat the scan using proper gel and placement. Expected outcome: The scanner resumes normal detection and scan confidence. If restart resolves the issue, monitor for recurrence and document the behavior.

steps.title: Check for Error Messages or Abnormal Indicators
steps.instructions: The scanner may show warnings that point toward the cause. Look for probe not recognized messages. Look for system error messages. Look for battery warnings. Look for scan failure messages. Look for a frozen screen. Look for unusual startup behavior. Look for touchscreen or button response issues. Expected outcome: Any displayed error or abnormal condition is recorded. If error messages persist after restart and external checks, remove the device from service.

steps.title: Evaluate Whether the Problem Is Repeatable
steps.instructions: Repeatability determines whether the device can safely remain in use. Test the scanner with proper technique on more than one appropriate patient or approved test method if available. Confirm whether low-confidence scans happen every time or only under certain conditions. Expected outcome: The failure pattern is clear. If the issue is repeatable despite proper technique, adequate gel, clean probe surface, stable battery, and correct settings, remove the device from service.

CCR.Complaint: Clinical staff reported that the Caresono PadScan HD Series bladder scanner repeatedly displayed low-confidence scans and did not reliably detect the bladder.
CCR.Cause: Found adequate battery charge, clean probe surface, proper gel use, and correct scan technique, but the scanner continued producing inconsistent bladder detection compared with another unit.
CCR.Resolution: Removed the PadScan HD Series scanner from service, labeled it Out of Service, and sent it for bench evaluation/probe and imaging system assessment.

helpfulDetails: Alarm behavior or scan failure message
Accessories swapped, including probe, charger, cable, or gel if applicable
Power behavior, battery charge level, charger status, and startup behavior
Environmental factors such as scanning over bedding, dressings, pads, or difficult patient positioning
Indicator lights, display warnings, frozen screen behavior, or probe recognition messages
Final device status, including whether the scanner was returned to service or labeled Out of Service

title: Caresono PadScan HD Series Bladder Scanner - Touchscreen Not Responding or Freezing
description: Troubleshooting an unresponsive or frozen PadScan HD touchscreen caused by power, screen contamination, software freeze, accessory, or charging issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not troubleshoot the bladder scanner while it is being used for an active patient assessment. Action: Remove the PadScan HD from clinical use and have staff use another bladder scanner if an immediate scan is needed. Expected outcome: Patient care continues without delay while the device is checked safely. If this resolves the clinical need, stop and document the device status.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Touchscreen Issue
steps.instructions: Confirm whether the screen is completely frozen, delayed, inaccurate, or only unresponsive in certain areas. Action: Observe the display and attempt basic menu selections. Note whether the device responds to any touch input, physical buttons, or power controls. Expected outcome: The failure mode is confirmed before assuming touchscreen hardware failure.

steps.title: Check for Screen Contamination or Moisture
steps.instructions: Bladder scanners are often cleaned frequently, and residue or moisture can affect touchscreen response. Action: Inspect the screen for cleaning residue, gel, disinfectant film, cracks, liquid, or debris. Clean the screen using approved facility cleaning methods and allow it to dry fully. Expected outcome: Touch response returns if the screen was affected by residue or moisture. If the touchscreen responds normally after cleaning and drying, return the device to service after function testing.

steps.title: Remove Gloves or Test With a Bare Finger if Safe
steps.instructions: Some touchscreens may respond poorly to certain gloves, wet gloves, or heavy pressure. Action: If infection control conditions allow, test the touchscreen with a clean, dry bare finger or a compatible stylus if available. Expected outcome: The screen responds normally if the issue is related to glove contact or touch technique. If this resolves the issue, educate staff and document the finding.

steps.title: Check Battery Charge and Power Status
steps.instructions: Low battery or unstable power can cause freezing, delayed response, or abnormal startup behavior. Action: Place the PadScan HD on its charger or connect the approved power adapter. Confirm charging indicators or battery status if visible. Expected outcome: The unit powers normally and touchscreen response improves after sufficient charging. If the issue was caused by low battery, allow the unit to charge and verify normal operation before returning it to service.

steps.title: Perform a Controlled Power Cycle
steps.instructions: A temporary software freeze may clear after a proper restart. Action: Power the unit off using the normal power button if possible. Wait at least 30 seconds, then power it back on. Expected outcome: The system boots normally and the touchscreen responds. If the touchscreen works after restart, perform a test scan or function check before returning the device to service.

steps.title: Check for a Frozen Application or Startup Screen
steps.instructions: Determine whether the scanner is frozen during startup, at the main menu, during scanning, or after saving results. Action: Observe when the freeze occurs. Note whether it happens every time, intermittently, or only after a specific function such as scanning, printing, exporting, or entering patient information. Expected outcome: The issue pattern helps separate a simple software lockup from a recurring system fault.

steps.title: Disconnect External Accessories
steps.instructions: Connected accessories can occasionally contribute to system lag or freezing. Action: Remove any connected printer, USB cable, docking connection, network cable, or peripheral accessory if present. Restart the scanner and test the touchscreen again. Expected outcome: Touchscreen function returns if an external accessory or connection was causing the system to hang. If the scanner works normally without the accessory, inspect or replace the accessory before reconnecting it.

steps.title: Inspect the Charger, Dock, and Power Adapter
steps.instructions: Poor charging contacts or unstable power may cause intermittent freezing. Action: Check the charger, dock, power adapter, and charging contacts for damage, looseness, corrosion, bent pins, or debris. Expected outcome: Charging hardware appears intact and the unit receives stable power. If damaged charging hardware is found, remove the affected accessory from service and replace or repair it.

steps.title: Check for Physical Damage
steps.instructions: A dropped scanner may have internal display or touchscreen damage even if the screen is still visible. Action: Inspect the scanner housing, screen, probe area, handle, and seams for cracks, impact marks, loose parts, or signs of liquid intrusion. Expected outcome: Physical condition is confirmed before returning the device to patient care. If damage is found, remove the scanner from service and send it for repair evaluation.

steps.title: Allow the Device to Recover From Environmental Conditions
steps.instructions: Extreme cold, heat, or moisture can affect screen response and system performance. Action: If the device was stored in a cold room, hot area, transport cart, or recently cleaned heavily, allow it to sit at normal room temperature and dry fully before retesting. Expected outcome: Touchscreen response returns if the issue was environmental.

steps.title: Perform a Basic Functional Check
steps.instructions: After the screen responds, confirm that the device is not only touch-responsive but also usable. Action: Navigate through the main screen, start a test workflow if allowed by facility practice, confirm buttons respond correctly, and verify the unit does not freeze again. Expected outcome: The PadScan HD operates normally without freezing or delayed touchscreen response. If function is normal, return the device to service and document the action.

CCR.Complaint: Clinical staff reported that the Caresono PadScan HD bladder scanner touchscreen was frozen and would not respond during patient setup.
CCR.Cause: Screen had disinfectant residue and moisture present, and the touchscreen responded normally after cleaning, drying, and restarting the unit.
CCR.Resolution: Cleaned and dried the touchscreen, power cycled the PadScan HD, verified normal menu response and basic function, and returned the scanner to service.

helpfulDetails: Battery level or charging status
Whether the unit was on the charger or running on battery
Whether the touchscreen was completely frozen or partially responsive
Whether the issue occurred at startup, during scanning, or after saving results
Screen condition, including residue, gel, cracks, or moisture
Cleaning performed before the issue occurred
Charger, dock, or power adapter condition
Any connected accessories removed during testing
Whether a power cycle restored operation
Whether the issue returned after restart
Final device status

title: Caresono PadScan HD Series Bladder Scanner - Battery Not Charging or Power Failure
description: Troubleshooting battery charging, power-on failure, short runtime, or charger-related issues on a Caresono PadScan HD bladder scanner.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not troubleshoot the bladder scanner during active patient care if the scan is needed immediately. Use another bladder scanner or approved clinical assessment method if the device will not power on, has low battery, or cannot complete a scan. Patient care continues without relying on a scanner with uncertain power reliability. A bladder scanner with unstable power may shut down during scanning or provide incomplete clinical information.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Power Issue
steps.instructions: Confirm exactly what staff are reporting. Ask or observe whether the issue is unit will not power on, battery will not charge, battery drains quickly, charging indicator does not appear, unit powers off during use, charger or dock appears damaged, or display flickers, freezes, or shuts down. The problem is narrowed to a charging issue, battery runtime issue, power adapter issue, or device power failure.

steps.title: Check the Wall Outlet
steps.instructions: Start with the simplest external power source. Plug another known-working device into the same outlet, or test the PadScan charger on a known-good outlet. If the charger begins working on another outlet, the issue is with the original outlet or power strip, not the bladder scanner. Return the device to service only after confirming normal charging and power-on behavior.

steps.title: Inspect the Power Adapter and Cord
steps.instructions: A damaged charger can prevent battery charging or cause intermittent power. Inspect the charger, power cord, and plug for bent prongs, frayed insulation, loose connector, crushed cable, exposed wire, burn marks, unusual heat, chemical or fluid contamination. The charger and cable should be physically intact with no signs of overheating or damage. Remove the charger from service and replace it with the correct approved charger if damage is found.

steps.title: Confirm the Correct Charger Is Being Used
steps.instructions: Bladder scanners may physically accept similar-looking chargers that are not electrically correct. Verify that the power adapter matches the PadScan HD Series charging requirements and is the facility-approved charger for this device. The scanner is connected to the correct charger. Remove the incorrect charger, connect the correct charger, and retest charging if the wrong charger is found.

steps.title: Check the Charging Port or Dock Contacts
steps.instructions: Poor contact can prevent charging even when the charger is good. Inspect the scanner charging port, docking contacts, or cradle contacts if applicable. Look for debris, bent pins, liquid residue, corrosion, loose fit, cracked plastic, or charger not seating fully. The charger or dock should connect securely without looseness or visible contact damage. Clean only according to facility-approved external cleaning practices. Do not scrape contacts aggressively or disassemble the unit.

steps.title: Reseat the Scanner on the Charger or Dock
steps.instructions: Charging issues are sometimes caused by poor seating. Place the scanner firmly on the charging dock or reconnect the charging plug. Confirm that the scanner is seated correctly and not tilted or partially connected. The charging indicator should appear, or the device should show that external power is connected. Allow the scanner to charge, then verify normal startup and battery status before returning it to service.

steps.title: Allow Time for a Deeply Discharged Battery
steps.instructions: A fully depleted battery may not power the scanner immediately. Leave the scanner connected to the correct charger for an appropriate charging period. Then attempt to power it on again. The display, charging indicator, or power LED should respond after the battery recovers enough charge. Continue troubleshooting external power components before assuming internal failure if there is still no response.

steps.title: Check for Battery Runtime Problems
steps.instructions: If the scanner charges but dies quickly, the battery may be weak or near end of life. Fully charge the scanner, then monitor whether it powers on normally and holds charge during typical use. The scanner should remain powered long enough for normal clinical scanning activity. Remove the device from service for battery evaluation or replacement according to facility process if runtime is poor.

steps.title: Observe for Abnormal Heat, Smell, or Swelling
steps.instructions: Battery and charging issues require caution. Check the scanner, battery area, charger, and dock for unusual heat, burning smell, swollen case, deformed battery cover, melted plastic, crackling sound, or intermittent power cycling. The device and charger should remain cool to mildly warm with no odor, swelling, or damage. Stop troubleshooting, unplug the charger if safe, remove the device from service, and escalate for repair if any abnormal condition is found.

steps.title: Perform a Controlled Power Restart
steps.instructions: If the device powers on but behaves inconsistently, a restart may clear a temporary software or power-state issue. Power the scanner off, disconnect it from external power, wait briefly, reconnect to the charger, and power it back on. The scanner should boot normally and display battery or charging status. Confirm the device completes startup and is able to perform a basic readiness check before returning it to service.

steps.title: Check for Recurring Power Failure
steps.instructions: Intermittent shutdowns should not be ignored. Gently move the device and charger cable while connected. Do not stress the connector. Observe whether the charging status drops out or the device powers off. Charging and power status should remain stable. Suspect a loose charging port, damaged cable, failing dock, or internal power connection. Remove from service if power cuts in and out.

CCR.Complaint: Clinical staff reported the Caresono PadScan HD bladder scanner would not power on and did not appear to charge when placed on the charger.
CCR.Cause: Troubleshooting found the charging dock contacts were contaminated, preventing reliable charging connection.
CCR.Resolution: Cleaned the external charging contacts, verified the charging indicator appeared, allowed the unit to charge, confirmed normal power-on, and returned the scanner to service.

helpfulDetails: Alarm behavior or displayed power messages
Accessories swapped, including charger, dock, outlet, or power cord
Power behavior, including no power, low battery, shutdowns, or short runtime
Environmental factors such as fluid exposure, storage location, heat, or charging area conditions
Indicator lights, charging symbols, battery level, or display behavior
Outlet tested or changed
Charger inspected for damage
Correct charger verified
Dock or charging contacts inspected
Battery level before and after charging
Whether the scanner powered on after charging
Any unusual heat, smell, swelling, or damaged plastic
Whether the battery drains quickly
Final device status: returned to service, charger replaced, or removed for repair

title: Caresono PadScan HD Series Bladder Scanner - Probe communication or scan trigger failure
description: Troubleshooting probe communication, scan trigger, cable, connection, docking, or control issues preventing bladder scan capture.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not troubleshoot the bladder scanner while it is actively being used for patient assessment. Remove the scanner from patient use if the probe does not communicate, the scan button does not respond, or scan results cannot be trusted. Use another bladder scanner or alternate approved clinical method if bladder volume measurement is needed. Expected outcome: Patient care is not delayed or based on unreliable scan data. Why it matters: A failed probe or scan trigger can prevent accurate bladder volume measurement and may affect care decisions.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Problem
steps.instructions: Confirm exactly what is failing before assuming a probe defect. Power on the PadScan HD system. Attempt a scan using normal workflow. Observe whether the probe is not detected. Observe whether the scan trigger does not respond. Observe whether the scan starts but immediately stops. Observe whether the system displays a probe communication message. Observe whether the probe works intermittently. Observe whether the issue occurs only when the cable is moved. Expected outcome: The failure mode is clearly identified. If the scanner operates normally during testing, document that the issue could not be duplicated and return the device only if it passes functional checks.

steps.title: Check Battery Charge and System Power
steps.instructions: Low battery or unstable power can cause communication or scan initiation problems. Confirm the scanner has adequate battery charge. Connect the unit to its charger or power adapter if available. Allow the device to charge briefly if the battery is low. Restart the scanner and retest the probe. Expected outcome: The scanner powers normally and the probe responds to scan commands. If the problem resolves after charging, document low battery or power-related behavior and stop.

steps.title: Inspect the Probe and Cable Externally
steps.instructions: Probe communication failures are commonly caused by damaged cable strain relief, connector damage, or fluid contamination. Inspect the probe body for cracks, impact damage, loose housing, or signs of fluid intrusion. Inspect the cable along its full length for cuts, kinks, pulled insulation, or crushed areas. Check the probe strain relief at both ends. Do not flex or stress a visibly damaged cable during patient-use testing. Expected outcome: The probe and cable appear physically intact. If damage is found, remove the device from service and send it for repair or probe replacement evaluation.

steps.title: Check the Probe Connection
steps.instructions: A loose or partially seated probe connector can prevent communication with the scanner. Power the scanner off. Inspect the probe connector and scanner-side port for bent pins, debris, corrosion, gel residue, or liquid contamination. Reseat the connector fully if it is designed to be user-accessible. Power the scanner back on and retest. Expected outcome: The scanner recognizes the probe and scan trigger operation returns. If reseating the probe resolves the issue, document the loose connection and stop.

steps.title: Check for Gel, Fluid, or Debris Around the Probe and Connector
steps.instructions: Bladder scanners are frequently exposed to gel and cleaning fluids, which can affect probe buttons or connector contacts. Inspect the probe face, trigger area, connector area, and cable entry points. Clean external surfaces according to facility-approved cleaning procedures and manufacturer-compatible disinfectants. Allow the probe and connector area to dry fully before retesting. Expected outcome: The probe communicates normally and the scan trigger responds. If contamination was present and cleaning resolves the issue, document the finding and stop.

steps.title: Test the Scan Trigger or Button Response
steps.instructions: The scan trigger may be stuck, damaged, or non-responsive even if the probe itself is detected. Press the scan trigger several times and feel for normal movement. Check whether the button feels stuck, soft, cracked, loose, or inconsistent. Attempt a scan using any available on-screen scan control if the model supports it. Expected outcome: The scan trigger responds consistently and starts the scan. If the on-screen control works but the physical trigger does not, suspect probe trigger or button hardware failure and remove the device from service.

steps.title: Restart the System
steps.instructions: A software lockup may prevent the scanner from recognizing the probe or accepting scan input. Power the scanner off using the normal shutdown process. Wait briefly. Power the scanner back on. Allow the system to fully boot before testing the probe. Expected outcome: Probe communication and scan function return after restart. If the problem resolves after reboot, document the lockup behavior and monitor for recurrence.

steps.title: Check Docking, Charging, or Data Cradle Behavior
steps.instructions: If the PadScan HD system uses a dock, cradle, or charging base, poor docking contact may contribute to power or communication problems. Inspect the dock or cradle for damaged pins, debris, loose fit, or charging failure. Confirm the scanner charges normally when docked. Remove and reseat the unit in the dock. Retest after confirming stable power. Expected outcome: The scanner charges normally and probe operation is stable. If the scanner does not charge or power remains unstable, troubleshoot the charging system before replacing the probe.

steps.title: Compare With a Known-Good Probe or Scanner, If Available
steps.instructions: Swapping known-good accessories helps isolate whether the fault follows the probe or stays with the scanner. If another compatible PadScan HD probe is available, connect it to the scanner. If another compatible scanner is available, test the suspect probe on that unit. Only use compatible equipment approved by your facility and manufacturer configuration. Expected outcome: If the problem follows the probe, suspect probe/cable/trigger failure. If the problem stays with the scanner, suspect scanner-side port, interface, or internal hardware failure. If the known-good setup works, document the isolated failed component and remove it from service.

steps.title: Check for Recent Drops, Cleaning Events, or Repairs
steps.instructions: Intermittent probe problems often follow impact damage, liquid exposure, or recent service activity. Ask clinical staff whether the scanner was recently dropped, cleaned heavily, had gel spilled on it, or had the probe disconnected. Review recent work order history for repeated probe, trigger, power, or communication complaints. Expected outcome: Recent events help explain the failure and guide escalation. If the issue began after a drop or fluid exposure, remove the device from service for inspection.

steps.title: Perform a Basic Functional Test
steps.instructions: Before returning the scanner to service, confirm it can complete a normal scan workflow. Power on the scanner. Confirm the probe is detected. Start a scan using the trigger. Verify the system captures and displays a bladder scan result or expected test behavior. Confirm no communication error returns. Expected outcome: The scanner completes a normal scan sequence without probe or trigger failure. If the device passes, document testing and return it to service.

CCR.Complaint: Clinical staff reported that the Caresono PadScan HD probe would not communicate and the scan trigger would not start a bladder scan.
CCR.Cause: Found the probe connector partially seated with gel residue around the connector area, preventing reliable probe communication.
CCR.Resolution: Cleaned the external connector area, reseated the probe connection, restarted the scanner, and verified successful scan trigger operation during functional testing.

helpfulDetails: Alarm behavior or exact probe communication message displayed, if any
Accessories swapped, including known-good probe or scanner comparison
Power behavior, including battery level, charger use, docking, or charging behavior
Environmental factors, including recent drop, cleaning, gel, fluid, or debris exposure
Indicator lights, probe detection status, scan trigger response, cable condition, and connector condition
Final device status, including returned to service, removed from service, labeled Out of Service, or sent for repair

title: Caresono PadScan HD Series Bladder Scanner - Printer, export, or data storage issue
description: Troubleshooting PadScan HD printing, export, saved scan, storage, or report transfer issues caused by setup, media, cables, or workflow.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not troubleshoot printing, export, or data storage while delaying patient care. Action: Confirm the bladder scan result has been reviewed or documented by clinical staff before clearing, exporting, or restarting the device. If the scanner is needed for active patient care, provide another bladder scanner if available. Do not delete stored patient records unless approved by site policy. Expected outcome: Patient care and scan documentation are protected before troubleshooting begins. Why it matters: Printer or export issues are usually not life-threatening, but lost scan records can create documentation and care continuity problems.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Confirm exactly what function is failing. Action: Check whether the issue involves: Internal or external printer not printing. Blank or incomplete printout. Export to USB not working. Saved scans not appearing. Storage full message. Report cannot be transferred. Patient data not saving. Date/time or patient information missing from reports. Expected outcome: The failure mode is clearly identified before parts or settings are blamed. If the issue is only a workflow misunderstanding and the scanner prints, saves, or exports normally after proper steps are followed, document the correction and stop.

steps.title: Check Device Power and Startup Status
steps.instructions: Confirm the PadScan HD scanner is fully powered and operating normally. Action: Verify the scanner powers on without startup errors. Confirm the battery is adequately charged or connect the scanner to approved power if applicable. Restart the unit if the display or menu is frozen. Check whether the scanner responds normally to touchscreen or button inputs. Expected outcome: The scanner is stable enough to access stored data, print menus, and export functions. If a restart restores normal printer, export, or storage function, document the restart and successful test, then stop.

steps.title: Confirm the Scan Was Actually Saved
steps.instructions: Verify that the missing report or export file exists on the scanner. Action: Review the stored records, history, or patient list on the device. Confirm the scan was completed and saved before the user attempted to print or export. Check whether the user exited the exam before saving the result. Confirm the correct patient, date, or exam record is being selected. Expected outcome: The record is either found in storage or confirmed not to have been saved. If the scan was not saved, this is a workflow/documentation issue rather than a hardware failure. Notify the department and document the finding.

steps.title: Check Available Storage
steps.instructions: Look for signs that the scanner memory is full or near capacity. Action: Check for storage full, memory full, database full, or save failure messages. Review whether older records are preventing new scans from saving. Follow facility policy before deleting or archiving patient records. If records need to be removed, confirm clinical documentation retention requirements first. Expected outcome: The scanner has enough available memory to save new scan records. If clearing approved old records restores saving function, perform a test scan/save if appropriate, document the result, and stop.

steps.title: Verify Printer Paper or Label Media
steps.instructions: If the printer is not printing or prints blank, check the simple media issues first. Action: Confirm printer paper is installed. Confirm the paper roll is loaded in the correct direction if thermal paper is used. Check for jammed, torn, or incorrectly seated paper. Confirm the printer cover is fully closed. Inspect for debris around the paper path without disassembling the printer. Expected outcome: The printer has correct media installed and feeds normally. If reloading the paper restores printing, print a test record, document the correction, and stop.

steps.title: Check Printer Connection and Power
steps.instructions: If the PadScan HD uses an external printer, verify the printer is connected and powered. Action: Confirm the printer power cord or battery is connected. Check the printer power indicator. Reseat the printer cable or USB connection. Try a known-good cable if available. Confirm the printer is connected to the correct port. Verify the printer is not offline, paused, or showing an error light. Expected outcome: The printer is powered, connected, and ready to receive print jobs. If a loose cable or offline printer caused the issue, correct it, print a test report, document the result, and stop.

steps.title: Check Printer Selection or Output Setting
steps.instructions: Confirm the scanner is set to the correct print or output method. Action: Review the printer/export settings available to Clinical Engineering. Confirm the selected printer or report format matches the connected printer or export method. Check whether the device is set to print automatically or only after manual selection. Confirm the correct report type is selected. Expected outcome: The scanner is sending the report to the intended output path. If correcting the output setting restores printing or exporting, document the setting change and stop.

steps.title: Test Printing With a Known Stored Record
steps.instructions: Use a non-active or approved test record if available. Action: Select a stored record that is safe to print under facility policy. Attempt to print the report. Observe whether the scanner displays an error, sends the job, freezes, or does nothing. Note whether paper feeds, partially prints, or remains idle. Expected outcome: The printer behavior confirms whether the problem is scanner-side, printer-side, or media-related. If a test print succeeds, the reported issue may have been record-specific, workflow-related, or intermittent.

steps.title: Check USB Export Media
steps.instructions: If export to USB is failing, inspect the USB device first. Action: Confirm the USB drive is fully inserted. Try a known-good USB drive approved by the facility. Avoid using encrypted, damaged, unsupported, or very large USB drives unless the device supports them. Check whether the USB drive is full or write-protected. Confirm the scanner recognizes the USB device before exporting. Expected outcome: The scanner detects usable USB media and can attempt export. If changing to a known-good USB drive restores export, document the failed media and successful export test, then stop.

steps.title: Check File Format and Destination
steps.instructions: Confirm the user is expecting the correct export output. Action: Verify whether the scanner exports reports as images, PDFs, data files, or another supported format. Confirm the exported file is being checked on the correct USB folder or destination. Check whether the export completed but the user looked in the wrong location. Confirm the report contains the expected patient or exam information. Expected outcome: The export function and file location are understood. If the file exported successfully but was misidentified or saved in a different folder, document the workflow correction and stop.

steps.title: Check Date, Time, and Patient Information
steps.instructions: Incorrect date/time or patient identifiers can make stored records appear missing. Action: Verify the scanner date and time are correct. Confirm patient information was entered or selected properly before the scan was saved. Check whether the record is filed under a different patient ID, generic patient entry, or incorrect date. Correct date/time settings only if permitted by facility procedure. Expected outcome: Records can be located and exported using accurate identifying information. If records were saved under incorrect information, notify the department and document the finding.

steps.title: Check Network or Computer Export Path if Used
steps.instructions: If the PadScan HD is configured for network, PC, or database transfer, verify the external connection. Action: Confirm the network cable or Wi-Fi connection is active, if applicable. Check whether other devices on the same network are functioning. Verify the destination computer, shared folder, or receiving software is powered on and available. Confirm no recent IT changes affected IP address, permissions, firewall rules, or shared folder access. Escalate to IT if the scanner appears functional but the network destination is unavailable. Expected outcome: The scanner has a valid path to the intended export or storage destination. If restoring the network or destination system resolves the issue, document the external cause and stop.

steps.title: Review Error Messages or Indicator Lights
steps.instructions: Record any specific messages before resetting or escalating. Action: Document any displayed messages such as: Printer error. Paper empty. Storage full. Export failed. USB not detected. Save failed. Transfer failed. Database error. Expected outcome: The work order includes useful details for repair, vendor support, or IT escalation.

steps.title: Perform a Controlled Restart and Retest
steps.instructions: If no obvious external cause is found, restart the scanner and retest the affected function. Action: Power down the scanner normally. Wait briefly before restarting. Confirm the scanner boots without errors. Attempt one controlled test of the affected function: print, save, export, or transfer. Do not repeatedly power cycle if the device freezes, loses data, or shows storage/database errors. Expected outcome: Temporary software or communication faults clear after restart. If the issue resolves after restart and retest passes, document the restart and final status.

CCR.Complaint: Clinical staff reported that the Caresono PadScan HD would not export saved bladder scan reports to USB.
CCR.Cause: Troubleshooting found the scanner recognized a known-good USB drive, but the original USB drive was not detected and appeared to be the cause of the export failure.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced the USB drive with approved known-good media, successfully exported a stored test report, and returned the scanner to service.

helpfulDetails: Whether the issue involved printing, USB export, network transfer, or saved records
Exact error message displayed
Whether printer paper was checked or replaced
Whether printer cover was open or paper was jammed
Whether an external printer was powered and connected
Whether a known-good USB drive was tested
Whether storage full or database errors were present
Whether date/time and patient record lookup were verified
Whether network or destination computer issues were suspected
Whether the scanner restarted normally
Final device status: returned to service, sent to IT, or removed for repair
